title: Rourea fluminensis

intro: Rourea fluminensis, also known as the "pau-Brasil" or "Brazilian rouge," is a plant species in the family Ochnaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against oxidative stress.
